reasoning: The plan cleanly extends the proven harmonic-evolution architecture (layered sine curves per parameter) to four new structural uniforms. It follows existing patterns precisely: seeded harmonics in evolution.ts, new VisualParams fields in mappings.ts, GPU uniforms in the vertex shader for pointCloud, and CPU multiplications in particleField/waveField draw loops. The multiplicative-identity default (1.0) ensures backward compatibility. The cross-cutting VisualParams change is the main complexity driver but is well-managed by step 7's explicit test fixture updates.
testImpact: medium — 6 existing test files need baseParams fixture updates and T-012-12 passthrough assertion needs narrowing, plus 1 new test file with 8 tests

concerns.detail: The plan applies radialScale before deformation and fieldSpread before breathing scale. This ordering matters — applying fieldSpread after breathing means breathing and field-spread compound multiplicatively. Verify this produces the intended visual effect rather than applying fieldSpread before all deformations.

radialScale and fieldSpread modify base position (not velocity), so they are not gated by uMotionAmplitude. This is intentional per the plan but should be visually verified — structural drift at full amplitude while motion is reduced could feel jarring.

If harmonic drift pushes a value to its clamp boundary and holds it there, the evolution flattens temporarily. The chosen amplitude/range ratios make this unlikely but not impossible for twistStrength (range 0.1-2.5, potential drift ±0.75 from base 0.5-1.5). Consider soft-clamping or wider bounds if visual testing reveals plateaus.
Three new multiplications per particle per frame (600 particles × 3 ops) is negligible. No performance concern.

alignmentWithArchitecture.evolutionPattern: exact match — reuses CurveParams + harmonic layering + evalCurve
alignmentWithArchitecture.mappingsPattern: exact match — adds seeded base values via simpleHash, same as existing geo/time mappers
alignmentWithArchitecture.shaderPattern: exact match — new uniforms follow same declaration and piping pattern as existing 11 uniforms
alignmentWithArchitecture.systemPattern: exact match — pointCloud uses GPU uniforms, particleField/waveField use CPU multiplications, consistent with their existing draw() approaches
alignmentWithArchitecture.testPattern: exact match — new test file mirrors evolution.test.ts structure with range/smoothness/determinism/seed-variance checks
